advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase before cross-department learning begins and next phase promotion is granted.
key_learnings: The gut-brain axis connects intestinal microbiome health directly to mood regulation via the vagus nerve and serotonin production (90% of serotonin is synthesized in the gut). Deficiencies in omega-3 DHA, B vitamins (B6, B12, folate), zinc, iron, and magnesium are associated with increased depression and anxiety risk. Dietary quality scores predict mental health outcomes independently of socioeconomic factors.
research_findings: Mediterranean diet adherence reduces depression risk by 30–35% in prospective cohort studies. The SMILES trial demonstrated that dietary intervention (Mediterranean diet) produced remission in 32% of depressed patients versus 8% in the social support control group. Ultra-processed food consumption is associated with a 22% increased risk of depressive disorder in large-scale meta-analyses.
current_focus_areas: Developing nutritional psychiatry protocols to complement conventional mental health treatment; building dietary risk factor screening tools for depression and anxiety; researching the role of fermented foods and probiotics in mood regulation.

learning_log.entry: Assigned to the Therapeutic & Integrative Nutrition department under Department Leader Bot 16 (Anti-Inflammatory Diet). Received learning_start_directive: begin independent learning immediately and reach 100% before cross-department study begins. Reporting daily to Copilot as part of the Diet Specialist Team. Progress: 38%.
